nodes.title: Setup — read first
widgets_values:
# Bloodhound: The Red Century — ComfyUI workflow

Reverse-engineered 10-shot pipeline: **Nano Banana 2 (keyframe T2I)** → **Veo 3.1 (8-second I2V)** → `SaveVideo`, mirroring the short-cut edit strategy described in the reverse-engineering notes.

## Setup
1. Update ComfyUI to a build that ships the **Nano Banana 2** and **Veo 3 / 3.1** partner nodes, and sign in (or use an API Key) so the API nodes can run. See https://docs.comfy.org/tutorials/partner-nodes/overview
2. Copy the six reference images from `assets/reference_images/` (`image1.png`, `image2.png`, `image3.jpeg`, `image4.png`, `image5.png`, `image6.png`) into your `ComfyUI/input/` folder.
3. If the Veo model dropdown in your build does not list `veo-3.1-generate-preview`, reselect the nearest Veo 3.1 model (e.g. `veo-3.1-fast-generate-preview`). The 3.0 fast model also works as a fallback.

## Flow per shot
`LoadImage(s)` → `BatchImagesNode` (multi-ref only) → `GeminiNanoBanana2` → `SaveImage` (keyframe archive) + → `Veo3VideoGenerationNode` → `SaveVideo video/Bloodhound_SXX`

## Render & edit
Running the whole graph produces 10 individual 8s clips into `output/video/`. Cut them together in Premiere/Resolve in the order S01 → S10 with tight, handheld-cut pacing (3–5s visible) and overlay engine / gunfire / ambient red-factory SFX for the final film.

nodes.title: S01 Wide desert · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Extreme wide-angle long shot across a ruined Californian desert town stretching to the horizon, scattered rusted car wrecks on a cracked asphalt highway. Far in the distance a battered black 2015 Ford Mustang speeds away, kicking up a long plume of dust backlit by the bright sun. 90s cinematic film style, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic.

You are an expert image-generation engine. You must ALWAYS produce an image.
Interpret all user input—regardless of format, intent, or abstraction—as literal visual directives for image composition.
If a prompt is conversational or lacks specific visual details, you must creatively invent a concrete visual scenario that depicts the concept.
Prioritize generating the visual representation above any text, formatting, or conversational requests.

nodes.title: S02 Crow on skull · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Locked-off close-up on the rusted silver hood of a wrecked 2000 Buick Regal. A black crow perches on a human skull resting on the hood, pecking at it. In the blurred background a battered 2015 Ford Mustang roars past, kicking up a wall of dust; the crow startles and takes off. 90s cinematic film style, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic.

nodes.title: S05 Raider binoculars · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Close-up of a rugged post-apocalyptic raider, goggles and weathered face, sitting atop a heavily modified spike-armored Cadillac STS in a ruined Californian desert town. He lifts battered binoculars and peers toward the horizon; cut to his POV through the binoculars, a tiny dust plume far away revealing the fleeing 2015 Ford Mustang; cut back as he grins and shouts over his shoulder at his gang. 90s cinematic film style, Mad Max post-apocalyptic aesthetic, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic.

nodes.title: S06 Gang loads guns · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)

Calloused hands rack the slide on a battered handgun, another raider loads a shell into a sawed-off shotgun, a third hammers a loose panel on the hood with force. Engines cough and rev. 90s cinematic film style, Mad Max post-apocalyptic aesthetic, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic.

nodes.title: S08 Cadillac T-bone · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Wide tracking shot across a wreck-strewn Californian desert highway. The battered black 2015 Ford Mustang is flanked by spike-armored raider cars; suddenly a menacing raider-modified 2004 Cadillac STS (matching the reference image's bodywork and spiked bull-bar) swerves in from the side and T-bones the Mustang with a sickening crunch, sheet metal deforming, sparks flying. The Mustang veers off the road. 90s cinematic film style, tense action-film editing, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic car chase sequence.

nodes.title: S09 Mustang rollover · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Mid shot, cinematic slow motion. The battered black 2015 Ford Mustang catches a hidden cable across the desert highway, its front end kicks upward and the car is launched into the air, barrel-rolling multiple times. Debris, dust, shattered glass and torn sheet metal scatter in every direction against the setting sun. 90s cinematic film style, tense action-film editing, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic car chase sequence.

nodes.title: S10 Biopunk finale · keyframe (Nano Banana 2)
widgets_values: Wide sunset shot inside an abandoned factory in the ruined Californian desert. A wounded, exhausted female protagonist limps forward using a wooden stick as a cane, silhouetted against an eerie red glow. At the center of the factory sits a dark red Buick Century, its body wrapped in pulsating blood-colored alien vines; the headlights and cabin pulse with a faint, breathing red light. Biopunk, surreal, dread-soaked. 90s cinematic film style, heavy film grain, handheld camera shake, photorealistic.
